reason: Captured the continuous-send code-spec: accept-vs-dispatch split, single-active-run queue semantics, batch snapshot rules, runtime payload fields, error matrix, and required regressions.
Indexed the continuous-send runtime spec so future turn-queue changes read the dedicated contract before editing.
Documented that chat composer availability now follows runtime turn state instead of the transient send-request lifecycle.
Documented the controller rule for accept-and-schedule chat endpoints instead of waiting for the entire turn to finish inline.
Extended the continuous-send spec with observable queue-failure metadata and the guarded force-delete recovery path for idle failed queued batches.
Documented that failed queued batches surface in composer status and may enable an explicit confirmed delete recovery path.
Documented the narrow controller-side force delete override for idle queued conversations with recorded queue failures.
